notAvailable: Help for this module is not available.

menu.title: Digital menu — guide
menu.intro: This module lets you manage the digital menu, orders, bar and kitchen workflow, and the restaurant’s public website.

sections.title: Menu categories and items
steps: Go to Edit menu
Click + Category to add a new category (e.g. Starters, Fish, Drinks)
For bar items (cocktails, alcohol...) enable the Is bar category toggle — these items go to the Bar station, not the kitchen
Inside a category click + Add dish and fill in name, description, price and image
You can mark dishes as Daily special — they show prominently in the guest menu

sections.title: Orders
steps: In the Orders section you track all active orders in real time
Each order has a status per station: kitchen and bar are tracked separately
Order status: Received → in preparation (kitchen/bar) → Ready → Served
The waiter sees the status of each item — no need to call the kitchen to ask
The waiter Staff portal (/slug/staff) shows station statuses in real time — 🧑‍🍳 Kitchen preparing/ready, 🍷 Bar preparing/ready — identical to the admin view

sections.title: Kitchen and Bar — separate stations
steps: Kitchen and Bar are separate tabs in the admin sidebar, each with a realtime badge
The Kitchen shows only orders that contain kitchen items (without bar categories)
The Bar shows only orders with bar items (categories marked Is bar)
Kitchen and bar staff work completely independently — each sees only their own
An order is ready for the waiter only when both stations have marked their part as Ready

sections.title: Waiter requests
steps: Guests send requests from the menu (Call waiter, Bring bill...)
A new request appears as a red badge on the Requests tab in the sidebar
The waiter selects the request, writes a short reply and marks it Resolved
The messages guests see are defined in Menu settings → Messages

sections.title: Menu settings — tabs
steps: General: name, location, phone, working hours, description, brand color, QR session duration
Visibility: for each feature choose who sees it (Off / Registered / Everyone) — ordering, waiter, reservations, guest registration, hotel link, spa link
Messages: customize the messages guests choose for calling the waiter and the order rejection messages
Templates: choose the visual style of the guest menu from available templates — the change is instant

sections.title: Restaurant website
steps: In the sidebar click Restaurant website to open the visual editor of the public web page
The editor is split: form on the left, live preview on the right that updates as you type
Blocks (Hero, Story, Gallery, Specialties...) are toggled on/off and reordered by drag & drop
Each block has a layout choice (layout variants) and fields for content and images
Click Save to publish changes — the page is available at restby.me/your-slug/home

sections.title: Restaurant QR code
steps: Go to QR code for a general QR that leads to the menu without table info
Use it at the entrance, business card, social media or the menu
For table orders use the QR codes from the Tables module (each table has its own QR)

sections.title: Multilingual — content translation
steps: Enter dish names and descriptions, categories and messages in Montenegrin (the source language)
Content is automatically translated into the other languages (English, Serbian, Croatian, Albanian, Turkish, Russian) and shown to the guest in their chosen language
Translation is done by AI — to correct it, click the 🌐 button next to an item and enter your own translation (your correction is saved and AI will not overwrite it)
Static parts of the app (buttons, menus) are already translated into all 7 languages

tips: Bar categories are key for the correct workflow — every drink category should be marked Is bar
Templates and brand color together build the visual identity of the guest menu
A per-table QR (Tables module) is recommended for daily use because it tracks which table an order comes from
You can turn off ordering visibility for unregistered guests — useful for VIP views or demo mode
The restaurant website and guest menu are separate: the menu is for table orders, the website is a public web page
A waiter can charge an order to a room directly from the Staff portal (not only from the admin) — visible when the Hotel module is active

staff.title: Roles and permissions — guide
staff.intro: This module is for creating and managing roles. Assigning roles to employees is done in the HR → Employees module.

sections.title: Creating a role
steps: Click + New role or choose a template (Waiter, Kitchen, Bar, Manager, Reception, Spa therapist...)
Enter the role name
Permissions are grouped by module (horizontal tabs): Restaurant, Hotel, Spa...
Click Select all for one module or check granular permissions manually
Save the role

sections.title: Assigning a role to an employee
steps: Roles are assigned in the HR → Employees module
One employee can have multiple roles at once (e.g. Waiter + Bar)
Changes are instant — the employee immediately gets the new rights

sections.title: Staff portal (/slug/staff)
steps: Employees access the operational view at restby.me/your-slug/staff
The waiter sees orders with station statuses (🧑‍🍳 Kitchen preparing/ready, 🍷 Bar preparing/ready) and can charge an order to a hotel room (when the Hotel module is active)
A housekeeper sees housekeeping tasks and maintenance requests in real time — status changes (Start → Finish → Verify) are immediately reflected on the admin dashboard too
Reception can do check-in/check-out from a mobile device — the Front Desk page is mobile-friendly
No admin account needed — employees sign in with their own credentials

sections.title: Access security
steps: Employees see only the modules they have permission for
The owner always has full access
You can change permissions at any time
A role can be deleted only if it is not assigned to any employee

tips: A waiter typically needs: orders, waiter requests, table view
The kitchen needs: orders (kitchen view) — without access to other modules
A housekeeper needs: housekeeping tasks — without access to administration
Reception needs: hotel front desk, reservations, folio
A manager can have HR and analytics in addition to operational functions
The Staff portal link and QR code are on the dashboard (the “Staff portal” card) and under Staff → Staff portal

tables.title: Tables — guide
tables.intro: Drawing the table map, tracking status in real time, reservations and per-table QR codes.

sections.title: Drawing the table map
steps: Go to Table settings
Click Square table or Round table in the toolbar to add a table
Click the canvas to place the table where you want
Drag the table with the mouse to change its position
Click a table to edit its name, number of seats and shape
Use the resize handle (bottom-right corner) to change the size
Click Save map when done

sections.title: Waiter view
steps: Go to Table view for a realtime overview
Free tables are white, occupied orange, reserved blue
A red table with animation means a guest is calling the waiter
Click a table to see active orders and the total bill
The page refreshes automatically

sections.title: Reservations
steps: Go to Reservations to manage reservations
Click + New reservation and fill in guest details
On the map inside the form click a table to select it visually
Use the Calendar view to see which days are busy

sections.title: Online reservations
steps: Enable the Online reservations toggle in settings
Guests send requests at restby.me/your-slug/reservation
Each request has a Pending status until you approve it
Confirmed reservations show on the waiter map

sections.title: Per-table QR codes
steps: Click a table in the editor, then the QR code button
Each table has a unique QR that leads to the menu with the table number
Download and print — stick one on each table separately
When a guest scans and calls the waiter, the waiter sees exactly which table is calling

tips: A per-table QR is recommended for daily use — it tracks orders by table
The map saves automatically — guests and waiter see changes immediately
Reserved tables show in blue with times on the waiter map

inventory.title: Inventory — guide
inventory.intro: Tracking ingredients and finished products, inflows/outflows and automatic stock deduction on order.

sections.title: Adding inventory items
steps: Go to Inventory and click + New item
Enter the name, category and unit of measure
Set the current quantity and the minimum quantity (warning threshold)
Optionally enter the cost per unit to track expenses

sections.title: Stock movements
steps: Click ± next to an item for a manual inflow or outflow
In — new delivery, Out — waste, Adjustment — directly set a new quantity (stocktaking)
Each movement is recorded in the Movements history

sections.title: Recipes
steps: Go to Recipes to define which ingredients each dish consumes
Add ingredients and set the quantity per portion
When an order is received, stock is automatically deducted according to the recipe

sections.title: Warnings
steps: Items below the minimum have an orange indicator
Click the Low stock filter to see only critical items
Update the quantity via ± when new stock arrives

tips: Automatic deduction works only if recipes are defined for menu items
Adjustment is useful for reconciling the count after a physical stocktake
Spa consumables (oils, creams) are tracked in the same module — use the Spa category

hr.title: HR module — guide
hr.intro: Complete employee management: profiles, schedules, attendance, leave, payroll and reports.

sections.title: Employees
steps: Add an employee by email address — create an account right away or send a registration link
Enter wage details (hourly, weekly or monthly)
The employee profile has tabs: Basic info, Employment, Finances, Leave, History, Payroll
The role is assigned in the Employment tab — an employee can have multiple roles

sections.title: Work schedule
steps: Weekly view: click a cell to add a shift
One employee can have multiple shifts on the same day
Daily view: horizontal timeline (06:00–23:00) — yellow blocks are uncovered periods
Copy the previous week with the ↩ button for faster filling

sections.title: Attendance log
steps: The employee clicks Clock in and Clock out via the Staff portal
The admin can manually add or correct an entry for any date
The system automatically calculates total work hours per day

sections.title: Leave
steps: Types: Annual leave, Sick leave, Unpaid, Other
Each leave can be Pending or Approved — click Approve or Reject
Used annual leave days are calculated automatically

sections.title: Payroll
steps: Add items: daily wage, bonus, deduction, overtime or advance
Generate a payslip for the selected period
Payslip: Draft → Approved → Paid

sections.title: Reports
steps: Overview of work hours and costs per employee for the selected period
Export to CSV for further processing in Excel

tips: An employee must have an account with the same email to be able to sign in and see their own schedule
Roles are defined in Administrative settings → Roles and permissions
The Staff portal (restby.me/slug/staff) is the operational view for employees — without admin access
Use the daily schedule view for a quick check of shift coverage

guests.title: Guests — guide
guests.intro: A record of guests who registered via the guest menu, visit and spending history.

sections.title: Guest list
steps: Guests are automatically added to the record when they register via the guest menu
Each guest has a profile with basic details (name, email, phone)
Search guests by name or email
Click a guest to see details: order history, total spending and notes

sections.title: Hotel guests
steps: Guests who had a hotel reservation are automatically in the record
Hotel guests can access the Guest App (restby.me/slug/guest) with a reservation code
The Guest App shows: stay details, folio charges, room requests and spa booking

sections.title: Filters and search
steps: Filter guests by first visit date or total spending
A VIP tag can be assigned manually to guests with high spending

tips: Guest registration in the guest menu can be turned off in Menu settings → Visibility
Hotel guests are automatically in the record — no manual entry needed
The loyalty program (future phase) will use this base for points and rewards

analytics.title: Analytics — guide
analytics.intro: Overview of revenue, best-selling dishes, trends and detailed reports by period.

sections.title: Revenue overview
steps: Choose a period (today, week, month or a custom range)
Charts show revenue and order count per day
Compare with the previous period to see the trend

sections.title: Best-selling dishes
steps: A list of dishes sorted by number of portions sold
Click a dish to see details by day
Use this data to optimize the menu and inventory

sections.title: Export
steps: Report export (PDF/Excel) is in development — coming soon

tips: Compare revenue by day of week to identify the busiest periods
Revenue by category helps you decide where to invest in the menu
Hotel Revenue Management (RevPAR, ADR, Occupancy) is in the Hotel → Revenue management module

hotel.title: Hotel Core — guide
hotel.intro: Complete hotel management: rooms, reservations, availability calendar, front desk, folio, housekeeping, online booking and a Guest App for guests.

sections.title: Dashboard
steps: The dashboard shows an occupancy widget with the occupancy percentage and statistics
Arrivals today — all reservations with a check-in date today
Quick links: Front Desk, Reservations, Calendar, Rooms

sections.title: Rooms and room types
steps: Go to Room types to define categories (Standard, Deluxe, Suite...)
For each type enter name, description, max occupancy, base price and amenities
Go to Rooms to see a grid of all rooms — statuses: Free, Occupied, Cleaning, Maintenance, Blocked
Click a room to change its status or edit it
Choosing the "Cleaning" status automatically creates a housekeeping task visible in real time in the Housekeeping dashboard and Staff portal

sections.title: Reservations
steps: Click + New reservation: guest, room type, room, dates, price
The system automatically calculates the amount: number of nights × price per night
Status: Inquiry → Confirmed → Present → Checked out
The availability calendar shows 14 days in a Gantt view — click a free period for a new reservation

sections.title: Online booking
steps: Guests can book directly at restby.me/slug/book
The page shows available room types, prices and a booking form
An email confirmation is sent automatically on reservation
The booking link is also available on the public hotel page (/hotel) and the Guest App

sections.title: Front Desk — Check-in
steps: The Check-in today tab shows all confirmed reservations for today
Click Check-in ✓ — the system changes the reservation status to Present, the room to Occupied and creates an open folio
The Front Desk page is adapted for mobile devices — cards instead of a table on mobile

sections.title: Front Desk — Check-out
steps: The Check-out tab shows all present guests who need to leave
Review the folio before check-out
Click Check-out ✓ — reservation status → Checked out, room → Cleaning, folio → closed
The Front Desk page is adapted for mobile devices — reception can do check-in/check-out from a phone

sections.title: Folio system
steps: The folio opens automatically at check-in
It shows all charges: room, restaurant orders, minibar, spa, other
Click + Add item to manually add a charge
Quick buttons: 🥤 Minibar (minibar charge) and 🛍️ Sell product (spa retail)
Restaurant "Charge to room" orders are automatically added to the folio
Spa treatments paid via folio are added automatically
Close the folio at check-out — marks payment complete

sections.title: Minibar
steps: Go to Hotel → Minibar to define the price list (name + price): water, juice, beer...
Quick start: 📚 From library → select and import ready items at once
Charge per guest: open the guest folio → 🥤 Minibar → choose item and quantity
The item is added to the folio as "Minibar: <name>" and enters the balance
The minibar is not tracked by stock (refilled manually in rooms) — only the folio is charged

sections.title: Housekeeping
steps: Housekeeping tasks are created automatically at every check-out (room → Cleaning); they can also be created manually from the admin dashboard
Housekeepers see tasks in the Staff portal (/slug/staff) in real time — status changes (Start → Finish → Verify) are immediately reflected on the admin dashboard without refreshing the page
The admin tracks the status of all rooms in the Housekeeping dashboard
A housekeeper marks a room as Clean → the status automatically changes to Free

sections.title: Guest App for hotel guests
steps: Guests access the Guest App at restby.me/slug/guest
Login: email + 8-character reservation code from the email confirmation
The Guest App shows: stay details, folio charges, room requests (housekeeping, room service...) and spa booking
Room requests appear in real time on the Front Desk Requests tab

sections.title: Integration with the restaurant
steps: A waiter can charge an order to a hotel room — click Charge to room on a served order
Enter the room number — the system checks whether the guest has an open folio
The amount is automatically added to the guest folio
The "Charge to room" button also exists in the Staff portal (waiter module) — not only in the admin panel

sections.title: Revenue management
steps: The dashboard shows ADR (average daily rate), RevPAR and Occupancy Rate
Trend charts by week and month
Price plans: enter seasonal or weekday/weekend prices per room type
Export analytics to PDF/Excel

tips: The availability calendar is the fastest way to see occupancy — use it daily
The folio is created automatically at check-in — you don’t need to create it manually
Minibar and spa retail are charged directly from the guest folio (🥤 / 🛍️ buttons)
Housekeeping tasks are created automatically — housekeepers see them only in the Staff portal
The Guest App link is sent automatically in the reservation email confirmation
The public hotel web page (restby.me/slug/hotel) is edited in Hotel → Hotel website
The Reservations, Housekeeping and Revenue management pages are mobile-friendly — cards instead of tables on mobile devices
For quick initial setup use the “📋 Guide: Hotel” on the control panel (next to the Hotel title) — it walks you through room type, rooms and booking time
Online guest payments (booking/folio) are set up in Hotel → Payments (Monri for Montenegro, Stripe for testing)

spa.title: Spa & Wellness — guide
spa.intro: Complete spa center management: treatment catalog, therapists, rooms, booking, calendar and analytics.

sections.title: Treatments (catalog)
steps: Quick start: 📚 From library → import ready treatments (refine price and details later)
Go to Treatments to define the service catalog (massages, facial, wellness...)
For each treatment enter: name, category, duration, buffer between appointments and price
Mark which room types can host this treatment (treatment room, wet facility...)
Treatments with requires_consultation require a prior consultation at booking

sections.title: Therapists
steps: Go to Therapists to manage profiles — a therapist must be created as an employee in the HR module
Add specializations (deep tissue, hot stone, facial...) and languages
Link the therapist with the treatments they can perform
Exclude a therapist from availability with one click (e.g. sick leave or day off)

sections.title: Rooms
steps: Go to Rooms to define spaces (Room 1, Sauna, Hammam, Pool...)
Each room has a type (treatment_room, wet_facility, group...) and capacity
Treatments are automatically mapped to compatible rooms

sections.title: Calendar and appointments
steps: The Spa Calendar shows a Gantt by therapist — each row is a therapist, columns are time slots
Booked appointments are shown as colored bars
Go to Appointments for a list of all appointments with filters by date and status
Click an appointment to see details, notes or mark no-show

sections.title: Public booking
steps: Guests book treatments at restby.me/slug/spa
Flow: treatment selection → therapist (optional) → date and free slot → payment (folio for hotel guests, card for external)
Hotel guests can also book directly from the Guest App (Spa tab)
Email confirmation and reminder are sent automatically

sections.title: Spa analytics
steps: Overview of revenue per treatment, utilization rate per therapist and no-show rates
Ratio of hotel guests vs. external guests
Export to CSV format

sections.title: Packages
steps: Go to Packages to create combined offers (e.g. Romantic getaway: 2 nights + 2 treatments)
A package can include: room type, spa treatments and a description of what is included
Guests choose a package on the public spa page

sections.title: Reviews and ratings
steps: After a completed appointment, the guest rates the treatment (1–5 stars) — in the Guest App (Spa tab) for hotel guests
The therapist rating is automatically calculated as the average of all reviews
The average rating (★) is shown next to each therapist on the Therapists page

sections.title: Retail sales
steps: Go to Retail to define products for sale (oils, creams...) with price and stock
Sales go through the guest folio: open folio → 🛍️ Sell product → choose product and quantity
Stock is automatically deducted; the amount is added to the guest folio
Sales above available stock are blocked

tips: A buffer between appointments (e.g. 15 min) ensures the therapist and room are free for preparation
Folio integration: a hotel guest’s spa treatment is automatically added to the folio
External guests can pay for a treatment by card online (an active payment provider is required)
Spa link visibility in the guest menu is set in Menu settings → Visibility
A therapist must be an employee in the HR module to appear in the therapist list
The public spa page is visible to everyone — no hotel reservation needed
Retail products are sold from the guest folio (🛍️ Sell product); stock is tracked automatically

settings.title: Settings — guide
settings.intro: Basic venue details, logo and subscription management.

sections.title: Logo
steps: Upload a logo in JPG, PNG or WebP format (max 2MB)
The logo shows in the guest menu, admin panel and email notifications
Recommended format: square (1:1), min 200×200px
A white or transparent background looks best on all backgrounds

sections.title: Basic details
steps: Enter the venue name, location, phone, working hours and description
These details show to guests in the guest menu below the restaurant name
Changes are visible to guests immediately after saving

sections.title: Subscription
steps: Overview of the current plan and active addon modules
Subscription and billing management
Addon modules (Hotel, Spa) are activated here

sections.title: Fiscalization and invoices
steps: Found under Settings → Fiscalization (/admin/settings/fiscalization); available when the Fiscalization add-on is enabled

The „To issue“ section lists every served order, hotel folio and spa appointment without an invoice — click „Issue“ to create one. The list is permanent, so you cannot lose an invoice even if you forget to issue it right away
Turn on „Auto-issue invoice on close“ so an order invoice is created automatically the moment the order is closed
Click any invoice in the list to open and print it (Print button)
Note: connection to the tax authority (fiscal number, QR code) is coming soon; until then invoices are kept internally

sections.title: Currency and VAT rates
steps: Currency is set per property — all amounts and invoices use that currency (no conversion)
Assign a default VAT rate to each menu category
An individual dish or spa service can have its own rate that overrides the category
The invoice automatically splits net and VAT for each line item

tips: Menu visual settings (templates, colors) are in Digital menu → Menu settings → Templates
Feature visibility in the guest menu is set in Digital menu → Menu settings → Visibility
The restaurant website is edited in Digital menu → Restaurant website
The hotel website is edited in Hotel → Hotel website
Online payment (Monri/Stripe) is set up in Hotel → Payments; setup guides are on the control panel next to the Restaurant/Hotel title
The admin panel theme and colors (green/blue/purple + custom palettes) are set by the superadmin: /superadmin → tenant → Theme; dark mode is the ☀️/🌙 toggle in the sidebar
